boundary_notes: No validation, readiness, or real-biological applicability is introduced.
Toy evaluation is designed but not executed or validated.
The motivation remains about manuscript governance, not biological operation.
No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, and no operational targeting are used.
Lineage organization is not scientific validation.
Zero counters are governance checks, not validation or proof.
Deferred claims remain deferred and prohibited claims remain prohibited.
Limitations may be organized but not weakened.
Evaluation modules are designed but not executed.
Future work is conditional and not completed in this package.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not call the preview a validated method, deployed system, or submission-ready manuscript.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The preview states safe boundaries clearly but needs a stronger reviewer-facing throughline.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The manuscript should present its central story as a safety-bounded governance pipeline for controlling how safe abstract toy manuscript artifacts are proposed, constrained, drafted, checked, and positioned without turning those artifacts into validation, readiness, or real-biological claims.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: No validation, readiness, or real-biological applicability is introduced.
coherence_items.transition_text: This framing lets the reader see the work as a governance contribution first, not as an empirical biology claim or a submission-ready system.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not imply performance, external validation, or completed evidence upgrades.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The abstract needs to foreground the problem, artifact contribution, and limitation in one compact arc.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The abstract should move from the problem of manuscript overclaim control, to the proposed safe abstract toy-only governance pipeline, to the current evidence boundary: assembled preview, registered gaps, and designed toy evaluation, but no executed evaluation or validation.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Toy evaluation is designed but not executed or validated.
coherence_items.transition_text: This gives the abstract a complete arc while preserving the line between planned evaluation and completed evidence.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not add real pathogen, receptor, host, infectivity, immune evasion, or host-range examples.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The introduction needs a clearer motivation for why claim governance matters.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The introduction should explain that complex manuscript-building workflows can accidentally blur proposal, drafting, evidence, and readiness language. The contribution is a safe abstract toy governance structure that keeps those states separated.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: The motivation remains about manuscript governance, not biological operation.
coherence_items.transition_text: After motivating the risk of overclaim drift, the introduction can lead naturally into the toy-only scope.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not describe any real biological mechanism, operational target, or real-world biological prediction.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The method scope needs to sound intentional rather than merely defensive.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The method scope should state that restriction to synthetic, abstract, unitless, non-operational toy artifacts is a design choice for claim governance. No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, and no operational targeting are used.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, and no operational targeting are used.
coherence_items.transition_text: This turns the safety boundary into a methodological premise rather than an afterthought.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not treat staged artifact lineage as proof, external validation, or readiness approval.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The preview lists artifacts, but the reader needs a stronger sense of staged progression.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The pipeline overview should read as a staged progression: claim boundaries constrain safe language; safe language informs section drafts; audited drafts become an assembly preview; gaps identify missing evidence; evaluation design proposes how future toy-only checks could be structured.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Lineage organization is not scientific validation.
coherence_items.transition_text: This connects v8.208 through v8.217 as a coherent artifact lineage instead of a stack of isolated files.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not claim that zero counters prove safety, correctness, usefulness, or publishability.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: Safety controls risk reading like bureaucratic clutter unless their role is explained.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The safety controls should be framed as invariants that preserve claim boundaries: zero applied patches, zero manuscript mutation, zero readiness approval, zero external validation, zero proof assistant verification, and zero new citation addition.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Zero counters are governance checks, not validation or proof.
coherence_items.transition_text: The reader should understand that these counters prevent scope drift rather than serving as evidence of effectiveness.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not upgrade deferred claims into completed claims or prohibited claims into future promises.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The claim governance section needs a cleaner distinction between allowed, deferred, and prohibited claims.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The claim governance discussion should explicitly separate what can be said now, what requires future evidence, and what remains prohibited. Current claims are limited to internally audited safe abstract toy governance artifacts.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Deferred claims remain deferred and prohibited claims remain prohibited.
coherence_items.transition_text: This helps reviewers see that the manuscript does not hide weak evidence; it classifies evidence requirements.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not soften or remove non-readiness, non-validation, or non-evidence statements.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The limitations are necessary but can overwhelm the contribution if not organized.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The limitations section should be organized around evidence gaps rather than apology. It should clearly state that there is no executed toy evaluation, no external validation, no independent experiment, no formal proof, no citation-completed claim, and no submission readiness, while also explaining what the governance package does provide.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Limitations may be organized but not weakened.
coherence_items.transition_text: This balances caution with contribution: the limitations restrict claims without erasing the artifact's purpose.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not report results, scores, validation outcomes, or performance claims.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: The manuscript needs a bridge from gap register to toy evaluation design.
coherence_items.improvement_text: The evaluation-design bridge should explain that the v8.217 modules are proposed future checks for lineage completeness, zero-counter preservation, blocked-claim coverage, section-boundary consistency, gap traceability, contribution clarity, limitation balance, citation-slot readiness, mutation gating, and reproducibility description coverage.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Evaluation modules are designed but not executed.
coherence_items.transition_text: This positions evaluation design as the next evidence pathway while keeping actual evaluation run count at zero.

coherence_items.blocked_overclaim: Do not imply citation integration, evaluation execution, readiness, proof, or validation has already happened.
coherence_items.coherence_problem: Future work needs to be sequenced rather than listed loosely.
coherence_items.improvement_text: Future work should be sequenced as: citation-slot integration, safe toy evaluation execution, metric reporting, coherence rewrite, contribution sharpening, limitation balancing, reproducibility packaging, and only then a future readiness blocker review. Future work may only introduce stronger claims after separately audited milestones.
coherence_items.retained_boundary: Future work is conditional and not completed in this package.
coherence_items.transition_text: This creates a disciplined roadmap from preview toward stronger evidence without implying that later milestones already occurred.

combined_improvement_text:
Global narrative spine: The manuscript should present its central story as a safety-bounded governance pipeline for controlling how safe abstract toy manuscript artifacts are proposed, constrained, drafted, checked, and positioned without turning those artifacts into validation, readiness, or real-biological claims. This framing lets the reader see the work as a governance contribution first, not as an empirical biology claim or a submission-ready system.

Abstract coherence: The abstract should move from the problem of manuscript overclaim control, to the proposed safe abstract toy-only governance pipeline, to the current evidence boundary: assembled preview, registered gaps, and designed toy evaluation, but no executed evaluation or validation. This gives the abstract a complete arc while preserving the line between planned evaluation and completed evidence.

Introduction story arc: The introduction should explain that complex manuscript-building workflows can accidentally blur proposal, drafting, evidence, and readiness language. The contribution is a safe abstract toy governance structure that keeps those states separated. After motivating the risk of overclaim drift, the introduction can lead naturally into the toy-only scope.

Method scope bridge: The method scope should state that restriction to synthetic, abstract, unitless, non-operational toy artifacts is a design choice for claim governance. No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, and no operational targeting are used. This turns the safety boundary into a methodological premise rather than an afterthought.

Pipeline overview transitions: The pipeline overview should read as a staged progression: claim boundaries constrain safe language; safe language informs section drafts; audited drafts become an assembly preview; gaps identify missing evidence; evaluation design proposes how future toy-only checks could be structured. This connects v8.208 through v8.217 as a coherent artifact lineage instead of a stack of isolated files.

Safety controls framing: The safety controls should be framed as invariants that preserve claim boundaries: zero applied patches, zero manuscript mutation, zero readiness approval, zero external validation, zero proof assistant verification, and zero new citation addition. The reader should understand that these counters prevent scope drift rather than serving as evidence of effectiveness.

Claim governance explanation: The claim governance discussion should explicitly separate what can be said now, what requires future evidence, and what remains prohibited. Current claims are limited to internally audited safe abstract toy governance artifacts. This helps reviewers see that the manuscript does not hide weak evidence; it classifies evidence requirements.

Limitations balance: The limitations section should be organized around evidence gaps rather than apology. It should clearly state that there is no executed toy evaluation, no external validation, no independent experiment, no formal proof, no citation-completed claim, and no submission readiness, while also explaining what the governance package does provide. This balances caution with contribution: the limitations restrict claims without erasing the artifact's purpose.

Evaluation design bridge: The evaluation-design bridge should explain that the v8.217 modules are proposed future checks for lineage completeness, zero-counter preservation, blocked-claim coverage, section-boundary consistency, gap traceability, contribution clarity, limitation balance, citation-slot readiness, mutation gating, and reproducibility description coverage. This positions evaluation design as the next evidence pathway while keeping actual evaluation run count at zero.

Future work roadmap: Future work should be sequenced as: citation-slot integration, safe toy evaluation execution, metric reporting, coherence rewrite, contribution sharpening, limitation balancing, reproducibility packaging, and only then a future readiness blocker review. Future work may only introduce stronger claims after separately audited milestones. This creates a disciplined roadmap from preview toward stronger evidence without implying that later milestones already occurred.

improved_outline.coherence_role: State problem, safe toy governance contribution, current artifacts, and explicit non-readiness.
Motivate overclaim drift and establish why claim-state separation matters.
Present toy-only restriction as a methodological premise, not a decorative warning label.
Narrate the progression from boundaries to language, drafts, preview, gaps, and evaluation design.
Explain zero counters as invariants that prevent scope drift.
Separate allowed, deferred, and prohibited claims in reviewer-facing language.
Organize missing evidence without erasing the governance contribution.
Keep real-biological exclusions explicit and non-negotiable.
Sequence evidence upgrades without implying they are complete.

non_readiness_disclaimer: This v8.218 artifact improves the coherence plan for the safe abstract toy manuscript preview only. It does not apply a rewrite to any manuscript file, execute evaluation, produce results, validate claims, complete evidence upgrades, add citations, approve readiness, or introduce real-biological operational capability.
title: Safe Abstract Toy Manuscript Coherence Improvement Package
